headline: Medtronic Shares Slip Despite Earnings Beat as Fiscal 2027 Outlook Disappoints (MDT)
summary: Medtronic plc (NYSE:MDT) traded modestly lower in premarket activity on Wednesday after issuing fiscal 2027 earnings guidance that fell short of analyst expectations, overshadowing quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street forecasts. While the medical technology company delivered stronger-than-expected revenue and earnings for its fiscal fourth quarter, investors focused on a more cautious profit outlook for the year ahead.

content.title: Medtronic Shares Slip Despite Earnings Beat as Fiscal 2027 Outlook Disappoints (MDT)
content.summary: Medtronic plc (NYSE:MDT) traded modestly lower in premarket activity on Wednesday after issuing fiscal 2027 earnings guidance that fell short of analyst expectations, overshadowing quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street forecasts. While the medical technology company delivered stronger-than-expected revenue and earnings for its fiscal fourth quarter, investors focused on a more cautious profit outlook for the year ahead.

headline: Medtronic announces strategic investments to support future expansion of cardiac ablation portfolio as physician demand for Affera™ technologies broadens
summary: Medtronic plc (NYSE: MDT), a global leader in healthcare technology, today announced strategic investments in two privately held companies focused on the development of intracardiac echocardiography (ICE) catheter technologies. ICE catheters provide real-time, high-resolution imaging of the heart during electrophysiology (EP) procedures for cardiac arrhythmias such as atrial fibrillation (Afib). Medtronic intends to integrate ICE into the Affera™ mapping and ablation system to continue building

headline: How Medtronic’s Q4 Earnings Jump And 49th Straight Dividend Hike At Medtronic (MDT) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: In early June 2026, Medtronic reported fourth-quarter sales of US$9,807 million and net income of US$1,243 million, alongside a dividend increase to US$0.72 per share for the first quarter of fiscal 2027. This marks the company’s 49th consecutive yearly dividend increase, highlighting a long-running pattern of returning cash to shareholders even as it manages recent product recalls. We’ll now examine how Medtronic’s higher earnings and fresh dividend increase shape its investment narrative...

headline: How The Medtronic (MDT) Investment Story Is Evolving After A Steady Analyst Update
summary: Medtronic’s latest analyst update comes with no change in the stock’s price target, which keeps the formal outlook steady for now. Without a fresh adjustment to that figure, the focus shifts to how investors interpret a quiet move in a market that often reacts to even small tweaks. As you read on, you will see how to track these kinds of updates and what to watch as the Medtronic narrative develops over time. Wall Street's queuing for one rocket.
